frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Arre bhai, aajkal ki ladkiyan isko control samajh leti hain, par sindoor ya mangalsutra se hi to shaadi-shuda aurat suhagan lagti hai. Hamari parampara hai, aur sach bolo to aurat bhi tabhi zyada settled dikhti hai. Pehenne mein kya jaata hai, ghar ki izzat bhi bani rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as necessary for a married woman to look 'complete' or respectable, tying her identity to wifehood and family honor. It encodes tradition framing, completeness framing, and judgment of women who do not wear sindoor or mangalsutra.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
